title: THE DEAD ENGINEER — DIRECTOR'S CUT V4
episodeObjective: Guide Hale from the relay deck to the personnel cache, medical archive, and emergency carrier control point to determine why the station records say he died while his signal remains active.
directionRules: Every episode has a concrete objective.
Characters stay physically active and move through real spaces.
Every decision has a visible consequence.
Matching puzzles are minimized or removed unless dramatically necessary.
System messages are minimized and only used for terminal/telemetry events.
Trust is never displayed directly; it is expressed through dialogue.
The player always receives exactly two choices.
Characters can react negatively to each other and to the player.
Every character has a distinct voice.
The story feels like a live rescue operation, not a static chat.

removedPuzzles.hale_death_correlation_03: Removed. Replaced with a two-choice operational inference at e03_n09_timeline_conflict to reduce matching puzzle repetition.

e03_n01.speaker: HALE
e03_n01.objective: Move Hale out of the relay room and toward the personnel cache while confronting channel 7.14.

events.text: About channel 7.14...
Before we go near it, I need you to understand something.
Curiosity is how this station got most of its scars.
Scientists call it discovery. Engineers call it troubleshooting. Management calls it initiative.
The rest of us called it Tuesday.
I'm leaving the relay room now. Personnel cache is two decks down, medical archive is one deck past that.
If the map is telling the truth, we can get answers without touching 7.14.
The map has lied before.

e03_n01_ask714.speaker: HALE
e03_n01_ask714.objective: Hale explains the channel while continuing to move.

events.text: You are going straight for the thing I asked you not to touch.
I respect the consistency. I hate the survival instinct.

events.title: SERVICE LADDER
events.subtitle: Hale descends toward the personnel cache

events.text: 7.14 was a backup emergency carrier. Quiet frequency, low traffic, ugly signal profile.
The kind of thing you build because redundancy looks responsible in a report.
Then it answered before anyone transmitted.
A backup channel should not talk first.
If it answers us again, remember this was not a radio. It was a door handle.

e03_n01_file.speaker: HALE
e03_n01_file.objective: Hale agrees to check the file first and moves with better focus.

events.text: Good. Records first.
I hate that the unknown voice gave useful advice. That feels unfair.

events.title: SERVICE LADDER
events.subtitle: Hale descends carefully toward the personnel cache

events.text: For the record, if a haunted signal gives better procedural guidance than I do, I am filing a formal complaint.
Assuming the complaint department survived.
Personnel cache door is ahead. Half open. That is never charming.

e03_n02_cache_door.speaker: HALE
e03_n02_cache_door.objective: Choose how Hale enters the personnel cache.

events.text: I'm outside the personnel cache.
Door is jammed halfway open. Enough for me to squeeze through, not enough for dignity.
There's a manual crank on the wall and a maintenance panel under the frame.
Crank is faster. Panel is safer. I already know which one you'll pick and I'm ready to judge you silently.

e03_n02_force_door.speaker: HALE
e03_n02_force_door.objective: Hale forces the cache door and takes a physical risk.

events.text: Forcing it.

events.title: PERSONNEL CACHE DOOR
events.subtitle: Hale forces the jammed door open

events.text: I'm in.
Also, I pinched my glove seal. Not a rupture. Just the station reminding me it hates initiative.
Air hissed for half a second. My heart did something deeply unprofessional.
Cache terminals are still powered.

e03_n02_inspect_panel.speaker: HALE
e03_n02_inspect_panel.objective: Hale inspects the panel and finds a clue before entering.

events.text: Inspecting the panel.

events.title: MAINTENANCE PANEL
events.subtitle: Hale checks the cache door mechanism

events.text: Found a bypass strip. Someone used this recently.
Not station-recent. Human-recent. Scrape marks, glove residue, a tiny smear of sealant.
Whoever opened this did not want the door log to notice.
I'm inside. Cache terminals are live.

e03_n03_personnel_file.speaker: HALE
e03_n03_personnel_file.objective: Recover Hale's personnel file and force a psychological reaction.

events.title: PERSONNEL FILE: ETHAN HALE
events.content:
DR. ETHAN HALE
ROLE: CHIEF ENGINEER
ASSIGNMENT: ORBITAL RESEARCH STATION ECHO
STATUS: DECEASED
TIME OF DEATH: 21:20
LOCATION: DOOR 12 ACCESS CORRIDOR
events.caption: Recovered personnel record from corrupted station cache.
events.message: [PERSONNEL FILE: ETHAN HALE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Operator?
Read that again.
No. Don't. I read it.
I just needed the universe to make a different choice the second time.
Status: deceased.
I've been called a lot of things.
Chief Engineer. Workaholic. Condescending, once, by a man who used the word 'synergy' unironically.
Dead is new.
Do I sound dead to you?
That was not rhetorical. I am genuinely asking because my confidence in basic categories is collapsing.

e03_n04_confront.speaker: HALE
e03_n04_confront.objective: Hale becomes defensive but keeps moving toward medical records.

events.text: Yes. I can read.
I am trying very hard not to think about what that means while continuing to breathe.
Which is an extremely inconvenient thing to do while looking at your own death certificate.
There should be a manual for this. 'So You've Been Officially Dead for Months.'
I'm moving to medical archive. If this file is wrong, medical should contradict it.
If medical agrees with it...
Then I don't know what I am.

e03_n04_reassure.speaker: HALE
e03_n04_reassure.objective: Hale steadies himself and chooses to verify the record.

events.text: Thank you.
That is probably the least scientific comfort available, and right now I am choosing to treasure it.
I can hear the relay hum. I can feel the deck vibrating under my boots.
I am annoyed by you, which is historically a living-person activity.
So let's keep that theory alive for as long as we can.
I'm heading to medical archive. We verify this before I start haunting anything.

e03_n05_to_medical.speaker: HALE
e03_n05_to_medical.objective: Guide Hale through the route toward medical archive.

events.text: Medical archive is past the observation corridor.
Two routes. Main corridor is direct but exposed. Lab access is slower and probably full of things with warning labels.
I cannot believe I miss normal warning labels.

e03_n06_main_corridor.speaker: HALE
e03_n06_main_corridor.objective: Hale takes the faster corridor and is pursued by an unknown sound.

events.text: Main corridor. Moving.

events.title: MAIN CORRIDOR
events.subtitle: Hale crosses the exposed observation corridor

events.text: Something moved in the ceiling while I was halfway across.
Not a rat. Before you ask. Too heavy.
I did not look up. I am learning.
Medical archive door is ahead. My pulse is making editorial comments.

e03_n06_lab_access.speaker: HALE
e03_n06_lab_access.objective: Hale uses the lab route and finds a humanizing memory.

events.text: Lab route. Slower. Less ceiling horror, theoretically.

events.title: LAB ACCESS
events.subtitle: Hale moves through the abandoned lab access route

events.text: There are mugs still magnetized to the wall rack.
Lin's mug is here. Tiny cartoon moon on it. She hated that mug and used it every day out of spite.
I miss stupid things.
Bad coffee. Cold pizza. Someone complaining the gravity sim made their knees feel old.
Medical archive door is ahead.

e03_n07_medical_archive.speaker: HALE
e03_n07_medical_archive.objective: Recover autopsy fragment without a matching puzzle.

events.text: Medical terminal is intact.
I would like to state in advance that I hate every word in that sentence.

events.title: AUTOPSY FRAGMENT: HALE
events.content:
SUBJECT: ETHAN HALE
CAUSE OF DEATH: DECOMPRESSION TRAUMA
LOCATION: DOOR 12 ACCESS CORRIDOR
RECOVERY STATUS: BODY NOT RECOVERED
NOTE: VITALS TERMINATED AT 21:20
ARCHIVE INTEGRITY: 68%
events.caption: Recovered emergency medical fragment.
events.message: [AUTOPSY FRAGMENT: HALE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Body not recovered.
That's somehow worse than recovered.
If they found a body, at least the nightmare has a shape.
But not recovered means the station either lost me...
...or it found something it refused to call a body.
Listen to me. I sound like a man arguing with a filing cabinet.
This is exactly why I hated medical logs. They make tragedy sound organized.

e03_n08_memory.speaker: HALE
e03_n08_memory.objective: Hale describes fragmented memory while walking toward relay analysis.

events.text: I don't know.
No. That's not honest.
I know pieces. I just don't know if they're mine.

events.title: RELAY ANALYSIS BAY
events.subtitle: Hale walks while trying to hold the memory together

events.text: I remember the door. The handle. The warning light above it.
I remember the seal making this soft little click. Ridiculous thing to remember, isn't it?
Not the death. Not the person. The click.
There was shouting. Someone said my name like I had already done something unforgivable.
Then a pressure drop. Or the memory of one.
Everything after that is gone.

e03_n08_signal_signature.speaker: HALE
e03_n08_signal_signature.objective: Analyze the live signal and reveal 7.14 residue.

events.title: VOICE SIGNATURE SCAN
events.subtitle: Relay analysis compares Hale's live carrier against archive records

events.title: VOICE SIGNATURE REPORT
events.content:
ACTIVE SPEAKER: ETHAN HALE
VOICEPRINT MATCH: 84%
SIGNAL DELAY: NONLINEAR
CARRIER TRACE: 7.14 MHz harmonic residue detected
events.caption: Recovered voiceprint comparison from live relay traffic.
events.message: [VOICE SIGNATURE REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Eighty-four percent.
That is just high enough to ruin my day and low enough to avoid answering anything.
Nonlinear delay. 7.14 residue.
Wonderful. Even my voice is doing suspicious things now.

e03_n09_timeline_conflict.speaker: HALE
e03_n09_timeline_conflict.objective: Replace matching puzzle with a two-choice operational inference.

events.title: TIMELINE CONFLICT
events.content:
21:14 — Door 12 opened.
21:17 — Security override accepted.
21:20 — Ethan Hale declared deceased.
21:22 — Live communication signature still matches Ethan Hale.

Conflict: active communication signature exists after recorded time of death.
Secondary conflict: communication signature contains 7.14 MHz harmonic residue.
events.caption: Automated timeline conflict report.

events.text: There it is.
A neat little contradiction with timestamps and labels.
The system says I died at 21:20. The relay says I kept talking after.
And somewhere between those two statements, I am supposed to exist.
We don't need another puzzle. We need a decision.
Either the record is wrong, or I am not the only thing using my name.

choices.text: TREAT THE DEATH RECORD AS CORRUPTED.
TREAT THE LIVE SIGNAL AS COMPROMISED.

e03_n10_record_wrong.speaker: HALE
e03_n10_record_wrong.objective: Hale takes comfort in the player's interpretation, but the unknown signal undermines it.

events.text: Record corrupted. I like that theory.
It is emotionally convenient, which makes it suspicious, but I like it.
If the file is wrong, then I am alive and the station is broken.
That is almost normal.
I hate that it waits until I'm almost calm.

e03_n10_signal_compromised.speaker: HALE
e03_n10_signal_compromised.objective: Hale is hurt by the player's suspicion but continues the operation.

events.text: So I'm the suspicious one.
No. That's fair.
It hurts, but fair and painless rarely share a room.
If my signal is compromised, then every word I send you could be a door opening from the wrong side.
I need you to remember something.
If I start sounding too calm, too certain, too helpful... question me.

e03_n11_other_voice.speaker: UNKNOWN
e03_n11_other_voice.objective: Reveal that the signal is not only Hale and force a safety choice.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: THERE WERE TWO
I heard that.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I wish I hadn't, but I did.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: A dead engineer with no body. A living signal with my voice. A channel leaving fingerprints.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I would like to formally resign from being a mystery.
events.speaker: HALE

e03_n12_move_focus.speaker: HALE
e03_n12_move_focus.objective: Hale moves away from the archive before the signal worsens.

events.text: Right. Moving. Good.
Movement is useful. Panic is just cardio with worse branding.

events.title: LEAVING MEDICAL ARCHIVE
events.subtitle: Hale moves toward the emergency carrier control point

events.text: I'm at the carrier control point.
The channel is there. Not open. Waiting.
I do not like systems that wait.

e03_n12_listen_voice.speaker: UNKNOWN
e03_n12_listen_voice.objective: The player listens to the unknown and attracts more contamination.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: NOT ALONE

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: ASK WHO ANSWERED

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: ASK WHO WORE HIS VOICE
I heard enough.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: You listened to it. I understand why. I hate that I understand why.
events.speaker: HALE

events.title: SIGNAL NOISE
events.subtitle: Hale moves under worsening carrier interference
events.completeText: [HALE REACHED EMERGENCY CARRIER CONTROL / SIGNAL DEGRADED]

events.text: I'm at the carrier control point. Signal is dirty now. We brought some of it with us.
events.speaker: HALE

e03_n13_memory_of_714.speaker: HALE
e03_n13_memory_of_714.objective: Hale remembers the other voice and sets up Episode 04.

events.text: I remember something now.
There was another voice on the emergency channel.
Not the unknown one. Not exactly.
It sounded close. Like hearing someone through a wall and realizing they know your name.
It kept repeating one number.
Operator... I don't think we're opening a frequency.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I think we're about to meet somebody.
events.speaker: HALE

e03_open_714_early.speaker: HALE
e03_open_714_early.objective: Risk route into Episode 04: 7.14 opens early and future transmissions are contaminated.

events.text: [CHANNEL 7.14 OPENED WITHOUT STABILIZATION]

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: TOO SOON
No— I said stabilize it first.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: The channel is open now. We did not lose anyone. But we lost control of how it opens.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: There is a difference between unlocking a door and having something pull it from the other side.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: [FUTURE TRANSMISSIONS MAY BE CONTAMINATED]

e03_stabilize_714.speaker: HALE
e03_stabilize_714.objective: Safer route into Episode 04: stabilize 7.14 before opening.

events.text: [CHANNEL 7.14 MONITORING ACTIVE]

events.title: SIGNAL STABILIZATION
events.subtitle: Monitoring emergency carrier before opening channel

events.text: Good. Slow is good.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I never thought I would say that. I have mocked slow systems professionally.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: But right now slow feels like a handrail.
events.speaker: HALE
UNKNOWN
events.text: 7.14 IS NOT A CHANNEL
It is a survivor.
events.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: Operator...
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That wasn't a metaphor.
events.speaker: HALE

focus: Every choice has exactly two options
Hale moves through personnel cache, medical archive, and carrier control
Consequences expressed through behavior instead of visible trust messages
Psychological pressure, defensive reactions, and reassurance routes

v4Upgrades: Hale reports ETA and goes radio-silent during travel
Psychological progression from confidence to self-doubt
